result:
Fleshed petition written to `/workspace/nexus-mk2/.scratch/patron-anima-eval/eval-a/reps/2/patron-flesh/cli-flag-edit.md`.

Key calls made beyond the thin brief:
- **Named reader/decision/frequency** (#22) — author fixing a stale title, low frequency, not a hot path.
- **Fail-loud validation chain** (#2) — empty title rejected, omitting both flags rejected, unknown writ id rejected; no silent no-op.
- **Extended the existing command** rather than inventing a new one (#3, #26) — `--title` as a peer of `--body`, riding the same book-writing path (#9, #15).
- **Scoped out** `--status` / `--type` / `--parent` (they belong to other contracts), `$EDITOR` mode, and bulk edit (#23, #18).
- **Flagged #36 as the real risk** — deferred to the planner whether writs have `tags`/`labels` that should ship in the same petition, so we don't add `--title` now and come back ad-hoc for the next field.
- **Deferred sealed-writ policy** — `--title` should match whatever `--body` does, not re-decide.
